1.d: A few more centaur herds are thinned out.

2.r: Kill  enemies using profession combinations with another player.
2.d: Kills that involved at least one profession combo with another player.

3.d: Scrape these scavengers off the surface of Tyria.

4.d: Clip the wings of these she-beasts.

5.d: Enough of these massive drakes on Tyria's massive plains.

6.d: They should have used their eight legs to run away from tougher fighters.

7.d: If small things were meant to live, they'd have bigger teeth or faster feet.

8.d: All criminals eventually meet a dark end.

9.d: Always willing to lend a hand, or sword, for the common good. You go where the action is.

10.r: Have  coins in the wallet at one time.
10.d: Money is truly no object for you.

11.r: Earn  hero points by completing hero challenges or by gaining a level with a level 80 character.
11.d: Your range of skills is quite impressive.

12.d: You've explored the war-torn hills of humanity's home.

13.d: Continuing a valiant battle against dehydration.

15.d: Two helms really are better than one.

16.d: It's not polite to eat the dead.

17.r: Deliver  killing blows with a staff.
17.d: A staff has many uses—all deadly.

18.r: Deliver  killing blows with a sword.
18.d: A classic weapon that grows more effective in your hand.

19.r: Deliver  killing blows with a dagger.
19.d: A slim, elegant blade with so many uses.

20.r: Deliver  killing blows with a longbow.
20.d: Kill 'em before they reach you? Kill 'em before they see you!

21.r: Deliver  killing blows with a axe.
21.d: It's not the prettiest weapon in the armory, but it slices beautifully.

22.r: Deliver  killing blows with a scepter.
22.d: Nothing weaves a web of magic like a fine scepter.

23.d: Consider these winged rats cut down.

24.d: Even the creatures in the deep aren't safe from harm.

25.d: Devourers may be ugly, tough, and dangerous, but they're still prey for a skilled adventurer.

26.d: Become the enemy of the collective by killing dredge.

27.d: Fire, earth, lightning, water, and ice can't stand up to steel and magic.

28.d: Just goes to show: two heads are not better than one.

29.d: Send the spirits into the Mists so they can rest in peace.

30.d: Gut the scaly swimmers to catch an achievement this big.

31.d: The bigger they are, the harder they fall.

32.d: Hunting these formidable beasts has been a Tyrian pastime for centuries.

33.d: They should have worshipped you. At least they'd still be alive.

35.r: Kill  members of the Flame Legion.
35.d: Smoke out Baelfire's minions, and show them what real power looks like.

36.d: Don't worry. Demon blood is easy to wash out.

37.d: If they are so smart, how come they couldn't stop from getting killed?

39.d: These creatures used to be powerful and great kings. Now, dead.

40.d: Make it safe to go back in the water.

42.r: Kill  members of the Nightmare Court.
42.d: Haunt these evil sylvari like a bad dream.

43.d: Big and tough are only impressive if you're alive to show it off.

44.d: Hit them in the right spot and they make cool patterns in the mud.

45.d: The worst thing to happen to pirates since scurvy and sea monsters.

46.d: These leafy monsters are now mulch.

47.d: These birds of prey have met their predator.

48.d: These tricky hunters learned the hard way you were the most dangerous game.

49.r: Kill  members of the Sons of Svanir.
49.d: The only good ice dragon cultist is a dead ice dragon cultist.

50.d: Even trolls can't stand such brutal onslaughts.

51.d: Tangling with you was the last stupid decision these smart creatures ever made.

52.d: Slice these slimy things from top to bottom.

53.d: Zhaitan should have raised a better army.

54.r: Deliver  killing blows with a short bow.
54.d: You deliver pain from a distance.

55.r: Deliver  killing blows with a mace.
55.d: Blunt force trauma is your friend, and you enjoy sharing it with your enemies.

56.r: Deliver  killing blows with a focus.
56.d: Only fools see this as an ornament. Only a master knows how to channel magic through it.

57.r: Deliver  killing blows with a greatsword.
57.d: You know how to wield a finely crafted piece of forged brutality.

58.r: Deliver  killing blows with a hammer.
58.d: When you absolutely want to turn your foes into jelly, this is your weapon.

59.r: Deliver  killing blows with a warhorn.
59.d: The sound of death marches upon your enemy.

60.r: Deliver  killing blows with a pistol.
60.d: You dispense death from the palm of your hand.

61.r: Deliver  killing blows with a rifle.
61.d: You deftly mix precision and power with this two-handed weapon.

62.r: Deliver  killing blows with a torch.

63.r: Deliver  killing blows with a shield.
63.d: You know how to turn defense into offense.

64.r: Complete the human personal story step "Breaking the Blade," "The Commander," or "The Trial of Julius Zamon."
64.d: Through valor and a thirst for justice, you earned Logan Thackeray's trust.

65.r: Complete the asura personal story step "Gadd's Last Gizmo," "Defusing the Problem," "Here, There, Everywhere," or "The Things We Do For Love."
65.d: Your brains and brawn were tested, and you stopped an Inquest plot.

66.r: Complete the norn personal story step "Minotaur Rampant," "The Last of the Giant-Kings," or "The Wolf Havroun."
66.d: Today, you proved your strength by stopping a threat to the lodge.

67.r: Complete the charr personal story step "Deadly Force," "Ghostbore Artillery," "Operation: Bulwark," or "Quick and Quiet."
67.d: You brought heat to the Flame Legion.

68.r: Complete the sylvari personal story step "Beneath a Cold Moon," "Black Night, White Stag," "The Heart of Nightmare," or "The Knight's Duel."
68.d: You were awakened in the Dream and beat back the nightmare threat.

69.r: Complete the sylvari personal story step "A Vision of Darkness," "Eye of the Sun," "Shattered Light," or "Snuffing Out Embers."
69.d: Some victories are bittersweet, but they're victories nonetheless.

70.r: Complete the asura personal story step "Flash Flood," "Magnum Opus," or "Split Second."
70.d: Your quick thinking and actions stopped an error in your past from causing disaster in the present.

71.r: Complete the charr personal story step "Dishonorable Discharge," "In the Ruins," "Sins of the Father," "Thicker Than Water," or "Tribunes in Effigy."
71.d: You have faced down the past, proving once again your might and wits.

72.r: Complete the human personal story step "Ashes of the Past," "Liberation," or "The Ringmaster."
72.d: You dealt with your past, and now you may join the world moving forward.

73.r: Complete the norn personal story step "Preserving the Balance," "The Championship Fight," or "The Machine in Action."
73.d: You cleaved an embarrassment from your past.

74.r: Complete the asura personal story step "A Meeting of the Minds," "Lines of Communication," or "Rakt and Ruin."
74.d: Despite a setback, you find yourself ready to embark on a larger adventure.

75.r: Complete the charr personal story step "Ever Vigilant," "Research and Destroy," or "Whispers of Vengeance."
75.d: Your honor is restored. However, you must prepare for a greater danger.

76.r: Complete the human personal story step "Kellach's Attack," "The Queen's Justice," or "The Trap."
76.d: You saved Queen Jennah from an insidious plot.

77.r: Complete the norn personal story step "Assault the Hill," "Sever the Head," or "Silent Warfare."
77.d: You destroyed the Dredge weapon and brought a fellow norn back on the path of destiny.

78.r: Complete the sylvari personal story step "A Tangle of Weeds," "Dead of Winter," or "Flower of Death."
78.d: You made sure the dead stayed dead.

79.n: Some Must Fight, That All May Be Free
79.r: Complete the story step "Minister's Defense" or "Quaestor's Siege."
79.d: You have become a full member of the Vigil.

80.r: Complete the story step "Attempted Deicide," "Evacuation," "Set to Blow," "The Lost Chieftain's Return," or "Unholy Grounds."
80.d: You came to the aid of another race under threat by the dragons.

81.d: You liberated Claw Island from the undead and swore to strike back at the heart of Orr.

82.r: Complete the personal story step "A Sad Duty," "Delivering Justice," or "Liberating Apatia."
82.d: You faced your worst fear and emerged triumphant.

83.r: Complete the personal story step "Victory or Death."
83.d: You are counted among the great heroes of Tyria.

It will help locate the dead drops across Diessa Plateau and Wayfarer Foothills and then decrypt them for you.

539.d: Rytlock Brimstone meets Rox in the Imperator's Core, where the defense quorum is debating. He assigns her a mission for which she will need support.

With an Empty Hylek Flask in your inventory, mix all four ichors together to create the Ichor Alkahest.
Speak with Atlalli, the Mastery Alchemist at the Hunting Grounds in Timberline Falls, to learn his secret ichor extraction technique.
After learning the special technique, achieve a rank of silver or better in the Haywire Punch-o-Matic Battle adventure within the Rata Novus Lane of Tangled Depths to gather the chak's ichor.
After learning the special technique, defeat the Champion Wasp Queen near the Ebonhawke Delegation in Fields of Ruin to gather her ichor.

After learning the special technique, defeat the Champion Branded Devourer Queen inside the Infestation in Iron Marches or the Champion Giant Branded Devourer in Sniper's Woods in Fields of Ruin to gather the ichor.
After learning the special technique, defeat the Legendary Chak Gerent in Tangled Depths to gather its ichor.
With an Empty Hylek Flask in your inventory, mix the four spider venoms to create the Arachnid Venom Decanter.
Speak with Eda in Queensdale to learn her spider venom extraction technique.
After learning the special technique, defeat Gargantula inside the cave at Wynchona Woods in Harathi Hinterlands and loot her venom.
After learning the special technique, defeat the Toxic Spider Queen just north of the Togatl Grounds in Kessex Hills and loot her venom.
After learning the special technique, defeat the Canyon Spider Queen located inside the Sweltering Canyons of Fireheart Rise and loot her venom.
After learning the special technique, defeat the Enraged Spider Queen outside the mansion of Almuten Estates in Gendarran Fields and loot her venom.
Speak with Jett, along the southern road exiting Griffonfall in Auric Basin, to obtain the M-PEC.
With an Empty Hylek Flask in your inventory, mix the six jungle bacteria to create the Concentrated Maguuma Bacteria.

Activate the M-PEC before defeating a Champion Arrowhead at Arrowhead Vale in Auric Basin to gather its mucus.
Activate the M-PEC before defeating a Champion Stonehead near the Griffonfall Pylon in Auric Basin to gather its salivary stone.

Speak with G.O.X. 2.0 at Zinn's Office, located inside the Rata Novus Command Center in Tangeled Depths, to gain the Incomplete Catalytic Converter.
After obtaining the incomplete catalytic converter, gather the Blademaster's jungle dragon core by defeating Mordremoth's commander near the Central Blighting Tower in Dragon's Stand.
After obtaining the incomplete catalytic converter, gather the Axemaster's jungle dragon core by defeating Mordremoth's commander near the Southern Blighting Tower in Dragon's Stand.
After obtaining the incomplete catalytic converter, gather the Stavemaster's jungle dragon core by defeating Mordremoth's commander near the Northern Blighting Tower in Dragon's Stand.

Seek the spirit of the wild raven that haunts its hills and forests. Let it lead you on its path in your quest to attain the Raven Staff, the next step in your quest to attain Nevermore.

Channel this power over ice into your efforts to create the legendary precursor Tooth of Frostfang.

Gather the ore of these fallen stars and use it to craft the spinning armillary that will eventually rest in the heart of your legendary weapon. Do this to create The Mechanism, the next step of your quest to attain Astralaria.

2449.d: Power, regardless of origin, is only of use when it is properly mastered, and to control the elements, one must understand the nature of control itself. Consider the nature of control, those who control, and those who are controlled, in your quest to attain the legendary precursor Storm.

Spend time in a Peacemaker jail cell in Rata Sum.
Save Zephyrites from Inquest captivity in Dry Top.

Help the Pact gain control of the Temple of Lyssa in Malchor's Leap.

2450.d: The froglike hylek are Tyria's best alchemists, the masters of potion and poison. Take the role of the apprentice in discovering the secrets of their work to create research, the first step in attaining the Hylek Optimized Projectile Experiment (HOPE).

b.text: Purchased from Atlalli, the Master Alchemist in the Hunting Banks of Timberline Falls.
Speak to Atlalli, the Master Alchemist in the Hunting Banks of Timberline Falls to start the Hylek Poison's Test once you have crafted the Vial of Potent Hylek Poison.
The secrets to crafting a quality hylek poison can be found among the alchemy supplies hidden in the treasure room of the Tears of Itlaocol in Caledon Forest.
Harvested from Veteran Mosquitos in Caledon Forest.
Found growing alone near the vista of Lightfoot Passage in Straits of Devastation.
Found on the Hylek of Morgatl Grounds in Caledon Forest.
Speak to Atlalli, the Master Alchemist in the Hunting Banks of Timberline Falls to start the Hylek Medicine's Test once you have crafted the Vial of Healing Breath.
Recipe purchased from Matlal after successfully helping her collect truffles for her starving tribe in the Eukaryan Caves of Kessex Hills.
Offered for purchase by Cook Huelic after helping the Michotl tribe within the Michoan Marsh of Metrica Province.
Found in the Great Jungle Wurm's chest in the Wychmire Swamp of Caledon Forest.
Purchase from Fiona Hastings in the apiary near Claypool in Queensdale after successfully defending her beehives from hungry bears.
Purchased from Atlalli, the Master Alchemist in the Hunting Banks of Timberline Falls.
Speak with Nochtli in the Prospect Valley of Dry Top about how to find this rare aloe.
Speak to Atlalli, the Master Alchemist in the Hunting Banks of Timberline Falls to start the Sun God's Breath test once you have crafted the Sun God's Breath Flask.
Offered for purchase by Cuadinti after helping him prepare Southwatch Post near the Astrozintli Forelands in Sparkfly Fen.
Found in Champion Mayatl the Fierce's chest near the Temple of Ameyalli in Verdant Brink.
Offered for purchase by Atzintli after helping the Atzintli and Zopatl tribes battle the krait in the Ventry Bay of Caledon Forest.
Attained from the Bugs in the Branches adventure of Jaka Itzel in Verdant Brink by reaching a rank of silver or better.
Harvested from the Bloomhunger in the Swamplands Fractal.
Complete this book by recording the three Hylek Alchemy Chapters in your Hylek Alchemy Apprentice Tome by passing each of Atlalli's tests with the tome in your inventory.

2468.d: The power of the earth is not only in its strength but in its fluid nature, moving slowly as stone should not move. Collect and bond with the oozes that live in the heart of the world to solve their mysteries and create the legendary precursor The Colossus.

b.text: A container used to store and combine ooze essences to create a pet ooze. Recipe sold by Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs.
Use your Ooze Terrarium to house a tiny ooze gathered from blood oozes.
Use your Ooze Terrarium to house a tiny ooze gathered from blue oozes.
Use your Ooze Terrarium to house a tiny ooze gathered from red oozes.
Use your Ooze Terrarium to house a tiny ooze gathered from toxic oozes.

Created by combining ooze essences in the Ooze Terrarium.
Feed Ooze Booze to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze. Doctor Bleent in Metrica Province has been conducting brewing experiments on ooze.
Feed Growth Tonic to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze. Chieftain Seegor of Degun Shun has been known to offer the brew after helping his tribe.
Feed Mystery Meat to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze. A common food enjoyed by norn and charr alike.
Feed a Petri Dish Delight to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze. Gastronome Plikk has expanded her selection to include this traditional asuran delicacy.
Feed Murellow Stimulant Snack to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze. Rykskott has been known to use this snack while training Murellows in Dredgehaunt Cliffs.
Feed Experimental Remnant to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze. Rakkan offers this food for a reasonable price after defeating the Risen Broodmother in Cursed Shore.
Feed Grumble Cakes to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze. This delightful cake can sometimes be found in the Dragon Chest after defeating the Shatterer.
Created after feeding food items to your Tiny Chromatic Ooze.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and go into the lava at the heart of Mount Maelstrom.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and enter the icy grave while fighting the Claw of Jormag.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and get hit by the electrified ground while fighting the Golem Mark II.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and get flattened by a Stomper in the Molten Furnace Fractal.

Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and enter the radiation chamber in the Thaumanova Fractal.

Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and take Agony Damage in the Fractals of the Mists.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and get hit by the poisoned environment while fighting Old Tom in the Uncategorized Fractal.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and take damage in Tequatl's watery grave.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and be affected by Corruption while fighting the Risen Priest of Grenth.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and fall for 10 seconds without touching the ground.
Coat yourself with your Mystic Chromatic Ooze and get hit by lasers in the Spekk's Laboratory jumping puzzle.

2470.r: Complete the Corruption revenant specialization to unlock the Mistward Warboots collection.

2475.r: Defeat the Jade Maw without anyone in your party being hit by its beam attack.

2476.r: Collect these  items and skins to receive the reaper's greatsword, Dark Harvest.

2477.r: Complete  scale 2 fractal in the Fractals of the Mists through the portal in Lion's Arch.

2478.d: The power of Jormag and the other Elder Dragons seeped into the lands of Tyria during the long epochs while they slept. Upwellings of this power may be found throughout the land, for those who know where to look and how to capture it. Let finding this power be the first part of your journey to craft the legendary precursor Tooth of Frostfang.

b.text: Purchase the recipe for this jar from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs.
Use a Destroyer Jar after defeating the Megadestroyer in Mount Maelstrom to fill it with essence.
Distill your captured Essence of Primordus in the caves below the Dominion Killing Zone in Kessex Hills.
Purchase the recipe for this jar from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs.
Use a Corrupted Jar after defeating the Claw of Jormag in Frostgorge Sound to fill it with essence.
Distill your captured Essence of Jormag near the frost portal in the Drakkar Spurs region of Frostgorge Sound.
Purchase the recipe for this jar from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs.
Use an Evergreen Jar after defeating the Vinewrath in Silverwastes to fill it with essence.
Distill your captured Essence of Mordremoth at the Corpse Grove in Verdant Brink.
Purchase the recipe for this jar from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs.
Use a Crystal Jar after defeating the Shatterer in Blazeridge Steppes to fill it with essence.
Distill your captured Essence of Kralkatorrik in the branded waterfall above Lake Desolann in the Iron Marches.
Purchase the recipe for this jar from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs.
Use a Vile Jar after defeating Tequatl the Sunless in Sparkfly Fen to fill it with essence.
Distill your captured Essence of Zhaitan at the Gates of Arah in Cursed Shore.

2479.n: The Flameseeker Prophecies I: The Experimental Shield
2479.d: Tyria is a land of heroes and villains locked in eternal struggle. Attain the "Anthology of Villains," and record the tales of the defeat of the most dastardly of their lot. Meditate upon the nature of villainy as you take the first steps in creating the legendary precursor known as The Chosen.

b.text: Purchase this recipe from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs in Lion's Arch.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of the leaders of the Molten Alliance in the Molten Boss Fractal.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of Mai Trin in her boss fractal.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to examine the Broken Mursaat Statue at the top of the Vizier's Tower in the Straits of Devastation.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of Taidha Covington on Laughing Gull Island in Bloodtide Coast.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of Drakin Cinderspire in the Scorchlands of Iron Marches.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to examine the remains of Scarlet's ultimate machine in Lion's Arch.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of Draithor the Drill in the Blackroot Cut of Kessex Hills.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of the Krait Blood Witch in Viathan Lake, Kessex Hills.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of the spirit of the Demagogue in Aurora's Remains in Brisban Wildlands.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of the Terror-Seven Krewe Leader and her pet Fluffy in Mount Maelstrom.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of the Infinity Coil Commander in Mount Maelstrom.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of the Svanir Shaman Chief at the Heart of Corruption in Wayfarer Foothills.
Bring an Anthology of Villains to the defeat of Champion Frainn in the Great Oouo of Timberline Falls.

2480.d: Brilliance strikes like a bolt from the blue, and that electric shock of imagination applies to both wielding and crafting the mightiest of weapons. Gather the energies of Tyria to infuse them into a superior weapon in your journey to craft the legendary precursor Zap.

b.text: Purchase this recipe from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs in Lion's Arch.

Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from a chak energy attack.

Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from a Veteran Air Elemental by killing it within melee range.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from an aspect lightning crystal in Dry Top.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from a spark at Opticalium's lab in Metrica Province.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from the central turret in Vexa's Laboratory in Fireheart Rise.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from a rotating wall while fighting Frizz in the Aetherblade Fractal.

Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from a lightning field in the Inquest Golem Mark II battle in Mount Maelstrom.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from an Aetherblade Striker's channeled lightning attack.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from the turrets guarding the ramp in the Uncategorized Fractal.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from a lightning turret in the Thaumanova Reactor Fractal.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from the Jade Maw's beam attack in the Solid Ocean Fractal.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from First Mate Horrik's lightning attack in the Mai Trin Fractal.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from a turret inside Oola's lab after it closes.
Use a Capacitive Bottle to capture energy from energy projectiles flying from the central reactor of Spekk's Lab in Caledon Forest.
Use the Luminates Plant in Metrica Province to add illumination to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the wreckage of the Breachmaker in Lion's Arch to add ley-line energy to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the Thaumacore Energy Source in Brisban Wildlands to add some unpredictable energy to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the Ancient Energy Source in Sparkfly Fen to add more energy to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the Strange Energy Source in Timberline Falls to add some strange energy to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the energy beams at the top of the Second Aetherblade Site in Gendarran Fields to add secrecy to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the Metal Forest in Malchor's Leap to add pyrite to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the Heart of the Volcano in Mount Maelstrom to add fire to a piece of charged quartz.
Use the sunbeam in the Forsaken Halls in Dredgehaunt Cliffs to add solar energy to a piece of charged quartz.
Purchased from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs after training the Historian of the Armaments ability in the Legendary Crafting Mastery Track.

2481.n: The Dreamer I: The Experimental Short Bow
2481.d: The heart hunts on a lonely train, plagued by desire, sadness, and occasionally joy. It pervades Tyria and its people. Track down these moments of the heart throughout the land in your first steps to create the legendary precursor The Lover.

b.text: Find the letter meant for Dessa in the Raving Asura's chest in the Uncategorized Fractal, and bring it to Dessa.
Find a rare cactus flower while harvesting cactus in the Maguuma Wastes and bring it to Ikkhit'ch's friend, the Seraph Archer in Camp Resolve.

Purchase a Stein of Ancient Deldrimor Ale from Arcanist Vance in the ruins of the Granite Citadel in Dredgehaunt Cliffs after helping him study the Dwarven relics. Then bring it to Ogden Stonehealer in the Durmand Priory.
Bring a red iris flower picked from the Urban Battlegrounds Fractal to the grave of Gwen Thackeray in Ebonhawke.
Purchased from Wark after rescuing him from the infatuated harpy matriarch.
Bring the Skritt King a shiny orb harvested from the large glowing orb at the Wreck of the Golden Vanity in Cursed Shore.
Found in the chest in the Underground Facility Fractal.
Found in the chest in the Cliffside Fractal.
Found in the Spekk's Lab chest in Caledon Forest.
Dropped by enemies guarding their gardens.
Dropped by Otyugh in Fields of Ruin.
Give a black pearl, dropped by the risen in Orr, to Peneloopee in Lion's Arch.
Read about the quaggan love potion in the Durmand Priory.
Bow to the Sculptor's Memorial in Malchor's Leap out of respect for his great love.

2482.r: Complete  door event in Mad King's Labyrinth.

2483.d: Versatile, flexible, and strong; tentacles invoke a feeling of strangeness and otherworldliness. Meditate on the virtues of such tentacled beasts as wind riders and jellyfish as you take your first steps in attaining the legendary precursor Venom.

b.text: Harvested from the Reef Riders of Southsun Cove.
Harvested from the Breeze Riders of Maguuma Jungle and Ascalon.
Harvested from the Dune Soarer in Dry Top.
Harvested from the Wind Riders of the Maguuma Wastes and Ascalon.
Harvested from the Irukandji of the Solid Ocean Fractal.
Harvested from the common Jellyfish of Central Tyria.
Harvested from the Arctic Jellyfish of Frostgorge Sound, Snowden Drifts, and Lornar's Pass.

2500.n: The Bifrost II: The Perfected Staff
2500.d: Your first attempt at crafting the legendary precursor The Legend has been modestly successful but has not yet yielded the weapon you ultimately desire. This journey has only just begun. Find a teacher, research your procedures, and hone your skills before you try a second time.

2501.r: Follow Taimi's trail further into the jungle.

2502.d: Natural materials are the foundations of understanding, but creative alchemy requires creations unlike those found in the natural world. Gather these unnatural materials in your efforts to forge the legendary precursor Spark.

b.text: Acquire an icy core from the chest after defeating the Svanir Shaman Chief at the Frozen Maw in Wayfarer Foothills.
Gather the fiery core of the elemental formed by the cleanup efforts in the ruins of the Thaumanova Reactor in Metrica Province.
Acquire putrid ichor from the chest after defeating Tequatl the Sunless in Sparkfly Fen.
Acquire a corrupted ice shard from the chest after killing the Claw of Jormag in Frostgorge Sound.
Acquire swamp gas from the Mossman in the Swampland Fractal.
Obtain an Inquest Power Core from the chest after defeating the Inquest Golem Mark II in Mount Maelstrom.
Acquire an ever-burning ember from Drakin Cinderspire's chest in the Iron Marches.
Use the frigid energy of the Elemental Source in the Snowblind Fractal to form Flame Jelly out of a Fire Core and 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust.

Use the cold environment near the Frost Portal at Drakkar Spurs in Frostgorge Sound to prevent an explosion while mixing an Ever-Burning Ember with 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust to form Blasting Powder.
Use the Boiling-Hot Magma in the Volcanic Fractal to blend 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust with a Corrupted Ice Shard to form Cryo Dust.
Use the heat from the Heart of the Destroyer in Mount Maelstrom to fuse 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust with an Icy Core to form Gelid Paste.
Use the pressure found in the depths of the Aquatic Ruins Fractal to combine 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust with Swamp Gas to form Explosive Fumes.
Use the pressure in the depths of the ocean in the Iron Horse Mines of Frostgorge Sound to form an Unstable Miasma from a Mordrem Thrasher Gas Bladder and 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust.
Use the low-pressure environment of Goemm's Lab high above Metrica Province to safely combine 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust with Putrid Ichor at a Console to form a Volatile Compound.
Form caustic paste by combining 10 Piles of Bloodstone Dust with the elements from an Inquest Power Core in the low-pressure environment found at the top of the Uncategorized Fractal.
Use the cold environment found in the Underground Facility Fractal to safely react Flame Jelly with Caustic Paste at the ice pillar to form a Smouldering Concoction.
Use Cryo Dust and the Scalding Fumes from the Molten Boss Fractal to stabilize Unstable Miasma and form an Acrid Jelly.
Use the materials from the shattered waypoint in Vexa's Lab in Fireheart Rise along with Gelid Paste to congeal the Explosive Fumes into a Corrosive Paste.

2524.d: Confetti bombs. Sigils of Mischief. Laughter. Pies. These are the weapons of a master prankster, used to embarrass the mighty and the might-be alike. Master these malicious matters of mischief in your efforts to craft the legendary precursor known as the Chaos Gun.

2528.d: The bounty of Tyria's nature is immense but not without limit, and as such must be cultivated, encouraged, and most of all protected. Get in touch with nature of Tyria in your quest to create the Ravenswood Branch, the first of the precursors to the legendary staff, Nevermore.

2530.n: The Bifrost I: The Experimental Staff
2530.d: The Jotun Star, fabled relic of the ancient mystical race, was said to call down a rainbow to the land. The stone, and its power, was broken long ago. Help bring it to rest as the first part of your journey to craft the legendary precursor The Legend.

2534.n: The Predator I: The Experimental Rifle
2534.d: The rifle, whether it fires bullets or magical rays, is one of the most complex common weapons of Tyria. Understand its wonders by researching the technology of such weapons as you take your first steps in the personal journey to create the legendary precursor known as The Hunter.

2536.n: The Flameseeker Prophecies III: The Chosen
2536.d: Tyria is a land of heroes and villains locked in eternal struggle. Attain the "Anthology of Heroes," and record the tales of the greatest of their number, both from the past and the heroes of today. Consider the true nature of heroism in your efforts to craft the legendary precursor known as The Chosen.

2549.d: Find this lost Exalted below the city of Tarir.

2550.d: Take upon yourself the responsibility of raising a raven from egg to chick to adult, while you work toward taming the wild raven spirit you have already caught. With the blessings of the Great Spirit, Raven, these two are destined to join together, earning you the Gift of the Raven Spirit, the penultimate step to attaining the legendary staff, Nevermore.

b.text: Offered for purchase from Shaman Sigrytha after honoring the Spirit of Raven in Wayfarer Foothills.
Raven eggs have been known to fall out of their nests around the Heart of the Raven in Wayfarer Foothills.
Take your Raven Egg to the Ravenbeak Shrine in Lornar's Pass to gain the blessings of Raven.
Take your Raven Egg to the Raven's Wing Shrine in Dredgehaunt Cliffs to consecrate it for melding with the wild raven spirit.
Stand in the Shadow Behemoth's Chaos Rift in Queensdale with your Raven Egg to prepare it for the chaos of the Mists.
Bring your Raven Egg to the entrance of the Artesian Waters in Cursed Shore to bathe it in the water from the source.
Bathe your Raven Egg in the ley-infused waterfalls of the Tranquil Refuge of Deeproot Sink in the Tangled Depths.
Fly to the top of the forgotten pillar near the Broken Altar of Dragon's Stand to bathe your Raven Egg in the falling waters.
Bathe your Raven Egg in the mineral waters of Southsun Cove.
Purchase this recipe from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs in Lion's Arch.
Harvested from the Veteran Spider Queen hiding in the cave behind the houses of Darkwound Defile in Kessex Hills.
Purchase this recipe from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs in Lion's Arch.

After acquiring Raven's blessing, consecrating and treating your Raven Egg, building the incubation box, gathering the silk insulation, and fashioning the warming stone, take your Raven Egg into the caldera of Mount Maelstrom to give it the last blast of heat needed to hatch it.
Purchase a herring from Fisher Travis in Queensdale after helping him maintain the river, and then feed it to your raven chick.
Feed a clam to your raven chick.
Harvest the meat of a plains wurm and feed it to your raven chick.
Harvest the meat of a luminescent grub and feed it to your raven chick.
Feed a prickly pear to your raven chick.
Purchase this recipe from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs in Lion's Arch.
Purchase this recipe from Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs in Lion's Arch.
Show your raven your flying skills while completing the Highest Gear behind the Falls in Auric Basin.
Show your raven your flying skills by jumping off of the Precarious Perch in Verdant Brink and flying due west to the flaming airship wreckage.
Show your raven your flying skills by jumping off of the Arboreal Overlook in Tangled Depths and flying to the mushroom ledge across the way.
Show your raven your flying skills while fighting and beating Mordremoth in Dragon's Stand.
Feed a fish steak to your raven chick.
Purchase salmon roe from Fisherman Will in Queensdale after helping the fishermen of Beetletun, and then feed it to your raven chick.
Harvest a protein-rich mushroom cap from the mushrooms of the jungle and feed it to your raven chick.

Harvest a termite larvae and feed it to your raven chick.

Take your raven to Dredgehaunt Cliffs and traverse the Tribulation Caverns to prime your raven for melding with a tamed spirit.

Take your Primed Raven and a Vision Crystal to the Raven Shaman's Power Source hidden within Darkhoof Heights in Snowden Drifts and use it to meld your Raven with the tamed spirit you have cultivated.
Slay the evil Raven Shaman, Vilnia Shadowsong, to gain access to the Raven Shaman's Power Source hidden within Darkhoof Heights in Snowden Drifts.
Upon completing the spirit's tests of power, friendship, and putting mists items to rest, visit the Heart of the Raven to ponder Raven as you prepare to meld this tamed spirit with a primed raven.
Collect a Red Iris Flower from the Urban Battleground Fractal and bring it to rest at the Viewing Hill plaque in Plains of Ashford.
Bring the Ancient Svanir Relic to rest at the foot of Jora's statue in Wayfarer Foothills.
Found in the chest in the Snowblind Fractal.
Bring the Ancient Grawl Relic to rest in the sacred waters of the God's Hand in Fireheart Rise.
Found in the chest in the Volcanic Fractal.
Take the Ancient Dwarven Texts from the mists to Ogden so that he may put them to rest.

Shoo the rabbits of Shaemoor back into their pen.
Purchase from Cassie after helping her around Mepi's Moa Ranch in Queensdale.

Show your power over spirits by defeating the Ascalonian soldiers of Duke Barradin's army in the Battle in the Vault under the Old Duke's Estate in Plains of Ashford.

2556.d: As the hylek are masters of alchemy, so too are the asura the wizards of technomagical research. They understand the forces in play in their Eternal Alchemy and how to apply them to the greater world. Uncover their secrets in your work to attain Prototype, the next step in your legendary journey for attaining HOPE.

b.text: A female asura researcher was last seen north of Wanderer's Waypoint.
Since he went to an upper ledge to examine the Exalted portal, the asura researcher hasn't been heard from.
Someone overheard the Zintl boasting about finding the remains of a female charr explorer.
Echoes of a male charr explorer fighting a large colony of vampire beasts filled the canopy the other night.
A human explorer bailed from the airship too early. She was lost near Capitzel Grounds.
Heading east, the explorer was cornered by a jaguar. The human never made it down from the canopy.
An injured norn historian held off the bonebreaker so others could escape. Her sacrifice won't be forgotten.
A lower ledge broke his fall, but then the norn explorer was surrounded by Tengu scouts.
A sylvari historian got trapped along the northern cliffside of Tarnished Treetop. She had little chance to survive.
An explorer tried to find his way south and down from the canopy. The sylvari only found a Coztic ambush.

2637.n: Frenzy I: The Experimental Harpoon Gun
2637.d: Fishing in Tyria is no pleasant day spent safely on the shore, waiting for a fish to take the bait. It is fought on the fish's home turf, a battle of fisherman against fish. And the fish fight back. Consider this as you take your first steps in crafting the legendary precursor Rage.

2642.n: Kamohoali'i Kotaki I: The Experimental Spear
2642.d: They move through the sea, silent and remorseless. They are respected and feared for their unrelenting savagery. They are sharks. Consider the place of these great beasts in the world, for good and ill, as you take your first steps in creating the legendary precursor known as Carcharias.

b.text: Acquire a Living Crystal from the Earth Elemental in the Tangled Depths and use it to capture energy left over in the pillars after defeating the Vale Guardian in Spirit Vale.

Infuse Spirit Threads from Gorseval the Multifarious in Spirit Vale with ley-line energy released when defeating a chak gerent near the Ley-Line Confluence in the Tangled Depths. Combine 5 Energized Spirit Threads to create a Spirit Weave.

b.text: The queen of Kryta called for a ceremony that attracted many unexpected guests.
The charr have many Weapons but perhaps the only Weapon they need is the genius of their automobile engineering.
The sacrifices of the norn's greatest heroes spills out of their Steadings giving Karma to us all.
The pride of the Arcane Council must be the height of the asuran technologies; those precious gates of theirs.

4335.d: Combine 2 Lesser Vision Crystals with 3 Vials of Blood, 6 Sapphire Orbs, and 15 Globs of Ectoplasm in the Mystic Forge to create an Ectoplasm-Infused Vision Crystal.

b.text: This vision crystal is imbued with ectoplasm, enabling the user to see deeper through the veil.
First, in Fields of Ruin: Go where the mightiest of the walled city rest.
Then, in Fields of Ruin: At the top of an abandoned mine, discover precarious pillars of steel...
Then, in Fields of Ruin: Seek out shattered storms, a twisting tree, and roots deep in stone.
Then, in Fields of Ruin: A waterfall streams forth with a stone ceiling overhead.
Then, in Fields of Ruin: Find the southernmost point, where one is closest to the sands beyond.
Finally, in Fields of Ruin: Embue an Oiled Orichalcum Greatsword Blade to become something more—a blade of a different age, forged by one long passed.

4336.r: Help Bakkin earn renown and fire the Favor of the Colossus.

b.text: This vision crystal is imbued with ectoplasm, enabling the user to see deeper through the veil.
First, in Dredgehaunt Cliffs: Find warmth at a hearth close to Hoelbrak.
Then, in Dredgehaunt Cliffs: Seek a great cat carved in stone.
Then, in Dredgehaunt Cliffs: Deep in the mountain, steel scores deep into stone.
Then, in Dredgehaunt Cliffs: The dredge seek riches to the southeast...
Then, in Dredgehaunt Cliffs: Search high in the sky, atop a mountain of timber.
Finally, in Dredgehaunt Cliffs: Echoes end and a legend begins at Brandalf's Steading. All Bakkin needs is an Oiled Orichalcum Torch Head...

4337.d: Combine 2 Lesser Vision Crystals with 3 Vials of Blood, 6 Sapphire Orbs, and 15 Globs of Ectoplasm in the Mystic Forge to create an Ectoplasm-Infused Vision Crystal.

b.text: This vision crystal is imbued with ectoplasm, enabling the user to see deeper through the veil.
First, in Drytop: Look out over a fallen people...
Then, in Drytop: Rendezvous with a rabble-rousing rock.
Then, in Drytop: Deep underground, a light shines bright as the sun.
Then, in Drytop: Look where waters mend, high above the oasis.
Then, in Drytop: Far to the southwest, high in the sky, there is a place to see both Zephyrite past and future.
Finally, in Drytop: The ghost returns to the crash site, ready to imbue the power of her people into an Oiled Orichalcum Sword Blade...

b.text: "I seem to remember hearing that every village used to have one of these."
"I wonder if anyone tossed them into the water for good luck."
"Scrolls say heket used to live east of here."
"I've heard this man had an entire garrison named after him."
"Ntouka liked the dry desert to the north."
"These probably have no value except to those trying to scam people, but I still want them."
"I think you can figure out where you might find this."
"Djinn like shiny things. Might be a good place to start."
"The decree was supposedly issued near the great hall."
"Did you know there used to be a way into the Realm of Torment from the Reclaimed Chantry?"
"I can't believe that tree is still standing."
"The scroll trade route went straight from Jahai to Gandara. Search along that path, maybe?"

b.text: Spoke to Sonu in Sun's Refuge and accepted his request.
North of the Tree, one still floats; your favorite toy as a child, those red toy boats.
In a house in Venta, an old frame lies; its story appears to whomever cries.
Right is left, but next time it's wrong; in the courtyard you will remember its song.
Along the River of Rilohn sits a large orange tree; with a view of the fortress, true love's flowers be.
In a graveyard from the right, pay your respects to whom they are due; one, three, then seven hold the clue.
You became a military man, though Awakened wear no suit. North of Wurmhaven, find your sword and salute.
Your favorite fruit, let that set in; a farmland's tree holds the key, to those who threaten.

6074.r: Find the missing pieces of the zu Heltzer tapestry, spread across Tyria.
6074.d: "That was the day the Ministry of Purity attacked. We said our good-byes, and then scattered across Tyria to preserve the bloodline."—Countess Danika zu Heltzer

b.text: Someone in Lion's Arch of Canthan descent may know more.
There could be something hidden in the depths of the Durmand Priory.
There may be tengu outside the Dominion of Winds who could help.
It could be worth pursuing rumors of lost wreckage in the waters surrounding Seitung Province.
An official in New Kaineng City's Lutgardis Plaza might be able to help you.

6455.r: Bring Albax lucky items from throughout Tyria by solving his riddles.
6455.d: "Bring to me your world, for I have lost mine." —Albax

b.text: "Brave the dead land to claim the foot of the swiftest." Albax pointed north, toward the Cursed Shore of Orr.
"Find the former walker of the blasted land, and gain a symbol of new growth." Albax shrugged while talking of the Branded landscape of the Iron Marches.
"In homes in pale parchment hills, find the form of an old survivor in the hands of a young one." Albax smiled, thinking of the rivers of Timberline Falls.
"In lush lands wracked with waste, ask for that which glitters in their patron's gaze." Albax's eyes opened wide picturing the dangers of Mount Maelstrom.
"The noble bird has been swept from solitude into the hands of chaos." Albax hinted at the monks of Shing Jea.
"The porcelain feline beckons you toward service in the name of the savory." Albax mentioned the smell of food in New Kaineng City.
"Bathed in gold and hubris, luck itself will lead you to me." Albax described a special room of games in Arborstone.
"In the sea of green, among the scaly ones, a relic lies with the mistakes of one's past." Albax gestured at the Jade Sea all around him.
"A sacred weapon is held in the boughs of great trees, never to be turned against them." Albax spoke of the verdant sights of the Maguuma Jungle.
"The needy hold tight to a hope of plenty in a pitiless golden land." Albax cringed while thinking of the heat in the Crystal Desert.

6579.r: Participate in the Dragon Bash Arena, Dragon Bash race, Moa Racing, and the Hologram Stampede. Progress awarded varies based on the activity.
6579.d: Weekly achievement. Progress resets on Monday morning.

6580.r:
Dodge  incoming attack from enemies.

<c=@reminder>Dodging allows you to evade incoming attacks by spending endurance to roll out of danger, making you temporarily invulnerable. "Evaded!" will appear over your character when you successfully dodge an attack.</c>

6581.r:
Craft  item.

<c=@reminder>Items can be crafted at crafting stations in cities and guild halls once you've been trained by an appropriate master craftsman. Crafting uses the crafting materials you collect from gathering nodes and defeated enemies as you explore the world.</c>

6582.r:
Use your utility skills  time.

<c=@reminder>Utility skills are unlocked from the training menu in the Hero panel after reaching level 11 by spending Hero points, and they are equipped to the three utility slots on the right side of the skill bar. </c>

6583.r:
Defeat  of the world bosses in Bloodtide Coast, Blazeridge Steppes, or Harathi Hinterlands.

<c=@reminder>World bosses are challenging events that appear at specific times of day across Tyria. Defeat them to receive chests containing rare equipment and crafting materials. </c>

6584.r: Complete 12 Adventure Guide: Volume Five achievements.

6585.r:
Mine  ore with a mining pick from resource nodes in Dredgehaunt Cliffs, Harathi Hinterlands, or Blazeridge Steppes.

<c=@reminder>Resource nodes are clumps of ore, saplings, and plants that can be gathered using gathering tools. Nodes can be found by exploring the world.</c>

6586.r: Get to Trahearne without triggering any of the Mordrem blighting pods.

6587.r:
Complete  renown heart in Frostgorge Sound.

<c=@reminder>Renown hearts are marked with a golden heart icon on the map. Every renown heart is different, so check your HUD when nearby to find out how you can assist.</c>

6588.r:
Participate in  event in Sparkfly Fen, Mount Maelstrom, Iron Marches, Fireheart Rise, or Timberline Falls.

<c=@reminder>You can find events while exploring the world. Events in progress are marked by an orange circle on your map when you're nearby.</c>

Acquire a completed memory of Mordremoth by collecting enough memory fragments of Elder Dragons.
Defeat the octovine while wielding an Aurene-based legendary weapon.
Purify the lingering Mordrem aether at one of Aurene's focusing crystals located at the Inner Chamber in Tarir.
Return to Leivas to purchase an uncharged fragment.
Defeat Mordremoth in Dragon's Stand.

6591.r: Discover 5 points of interest in the Ruins of Orr, Frostgorge Sound, or Southsun Cove.

6594.r:
Use the weapon-swap button to switch your active weapon  time.

<c=@reminder>Weapon swap allows you to change between your equipped weapons to access different skills. This mechanic is unlocked at level 10.</c>

6595.r:
Complete  renown heart in Sparkfly Fen, Mount Maelstrom, Iron Marches, Fireheart Rise, or Timberline Falls.

<c=@reminder>Renown hearts are marked with a golden heart icon on the map. Every renown heart is different, so check your HUD when nearby to find out how you can assist.</c>

6596.r:
Create  item in the Mystic Forge.

<c=@reminder>The Mystic Forge transforms items in a variety of ways, including raising the tier of crafting materials and upgrading equipment. The forge can be accessed from major cities throughout Tyria.</c>

6597.r:
Earn experience to level up this character to level 70.

<c=@reminder>Experience can be earned by defeating enemies, contributing to events and renown hearts, completing story chapters, and crafting items.</c>

6599.r: Play  ranked matches during Conquest Season Thirty-Three.

6600.r:
Earn experience to level up this character to level 10.

<c=@reminder>Experience can be earned by defeating enemies, contributing to events and renown hearts, completing story chapters, and crafting items.</c>

6601.r:
Travel using a waypoint.

<c=@reminder>Waypoints allow you to travel quickly around the world. They are unlocked by visiting them once. You can teleport to any unlocked waypoint for a small amount of coin.</c>

6602.r:
Deposit  item in your account bank.

<c=@reminder>The Bank tab of your account vault allows you to store items outside of your inventory and transfer them between characters.</c>

6603.r: Play  ranked matches during 2v2 Season Seven.

6604.r:
Earn experience to level up this character to level 50.

<c=@reminder>Experience can be earned by defeating enemies, contributing to events and renown hearts, completing story chapters, and crafting items.</c>

6605.r:
Complete  map.

<c=@reminder>Map-complete rewards are special bonuses earned by visiting all points of interest, vistas, waypoints, hero challenges, and renown hearts on a map.</c>

6606.r:
Use  elite skill.

<c=@reminder>Elite skills are powerful abilities assigned to the last slot of your skill bar.</c>

6607.r: Defeat  centaur, Sons of Svanir, Inquest, Nightmare Court, or Flame Legion enemy.

6608.r:
Earn 30 crafting levels.

<c=@reminder>Craft items to earn crafting experience for that crafting discipline, allowing you to create more powerful items.</c>

6627.r:
Dodge  incoming attack from enemies.

<c=@reminder>Dodging allows you to evade incoming attacks by spending endurance to roll out of danger, making you temporarily invulnerable. "Evaded!" will appear over your character when you successfully dodge an attack.</c>

Complete chapter 1 of your story.

<c=@reminder>You can select your active story and view your progress from the Story Journal menu in the Hero panel.</c>

Revive  ally.

<c=@reminder>Downed or defeated players and allies can be revived by approaching and interacting with them to restore their health. Multiple people can revive the same ally at once to restore health faster.</c>

Apply  conditions to enemies.

Complete chapter 3 of your story.

6640.r:
Use  combo skill.

6641.r:
Use  consumable food item to obtain nourishment bonuses.

<c=@reminder>Food consumables boost attributes when eaten. Hover your mouse over a consumable to see what it does.</c>

6642.r:
Transmute  item.

<c=@reminder>Transmutation expends transmutation charges to replace the skin of a piece of equipment with a different one from your account wardrobe. Transmutation charges can be found in daily reward chests, earned by completing maps, or purchased from the Gem Store.</c>

b.text: Dadga needs to speak with you in the Wizard's Tower.
All convoys must have a starting point, just as all climbs must have a place where you can start your ascent.

Flying books, treaties on centaur mating rituals, six hundred and fifty-seven thousand, eight hundred and ninety-one words about the uses of moa feathers can be found here. What else could be found between the shelves?
Someone in Amnytas is going beyond just closing rifts, but one must weigh the pros and cons of probing the unknown. Help them survive the ordeal.
Reports of wizard magic in Frostgorge Sound, just the smallest drop in a sea. A sorrowful one has found a prize...
Nobody knows what is stashed in the depths of deepest halls of knowledge, but someone in Amnytas is trying to find out...
Skywatch Archipelago is the gateway to many realities, but it would be folly to assume this tower was "the" tower.
The Kryptis flood the skies; the Heart thrums whenever they are near. Hunt down their incursions across the bastions or fractals and find your prize
Wizards fight with more than spells and swords—they concoct all sorts of brews as well. Seek out and aid this master while they test their creations.
Many things whisper in Amnytas that should not: doors, books, and spirits. Hunt them down and trace their source.
Buried in Rata Sum are many labs, but this one is so full of memory, it hurts.
Among the fractals of the archipelago, enormous amounts of emotional energy is coalescing. Help the gatherers and find your answers.
Magic can be used to heal, transmute, and inspire, but for this bastion, they focus on battle. Learn the poses and complete the spell.
If only magical barriers were as easy to maintain as waving a wand. It takes work and undying focus, and these barriers must not fall.
The queen's shadows meet in secret under the Shadow of Grenth.
A hobby that requires only two things: patience and water. Maybe a clue can be found if one also takes the practice?
Rifts let things through and others in. Search the fields where the fiend vanished, and someone nearby may have some answers.
The tower climbs high and holds infinite secrets. If one climbs high enough, maybe those secrets will reveal themselves...
If one is looking for the stars, there is only one bastion where they are found.
The charr have always shunned the rejects in their citadel; search the shadows thoroughly and cast a light to those creatures in the darkness.
Magic in Amnytas is always temperamental, but with the Kryptis attack it's running amok. Once statuesque, this place needs a helping hand.
Amnytas is suffering, but those who seek balance are suffering worst of all. Why debate it? Break open the hall and help them find peace.
The beast emerges, the spire entwined... Put out the eyes, each and every one.
Now that you have found every card, return to Dagda in the Wizard's Tower.

An esoteric artifact of a bygone age, it was originally designed to transfer essence past emotional blockages via constant and gradual microtransmutations.

It was shattered and discarded when someone asked the inventor, "Why don't you just tell them how you feel instead?"

Purchase from provisioners in major cities, the Maguuma Jungle, or the Horn of Maguuma.

Lyhr in the Wizard's Tower can also help create this.

Purchased from master craftspeople for research notes.

Essence of luck can be acquired by salvaging gear of fine or masterwork quality and salvaging globs of ectoplasm. Artificers can craft essence of luck at higher quality.
Lyhr in the Wizard's Tower can also help create this.

b.text: 1. Speak to the Kryptis delegation and accept their request.
2. Dash through five apple trees in Queensdale on your skyscale.
3. Rummage for ramen around Jade Mech Habitation Zone 03.
4. Convince Sous-Chef Seimur Oxbone to give you some bloodstone condiments. Speak with Astral Ward Culinary Specialist Torchmarrow in the Wizard's Tower to track where the sous-chef is each day.
5. Obtain some healthy (and edible) greenery from Narcisse in the Botanical Skygarden in Amnytas.
6. Obtain a keg of norn ale from Kevach's Homestead in Wayfarer Foothills.
7. Convince Grand Archivist Vivienne in the Wizard's Vault to give you a crate of ancient eggs.
8. Shoot the crate of ancient eggs that flies above Maelstrom's Core in Mount Maelstrom with your skyscale's Fireball skill, and pick the eggs out of the magma below before they burn.
9. Defeat 5 crabs with your skyscale's Fireball skill in Bloodtide Coast to flash-sear their meat.
10. Obtain a chunk of karka carapace from inside Bakestone Cavern in Southsun Cove.
11. Return to the Kryptis delegation after completing all previous steps to witness their feast.

b.text: 1. Speak with Kosmas near the Forward Bivouac Waypoint.
2. "Close to coliseum, near house! Part collapsed, trapped under rocks!"
4. "Near north extractor, saw creek. Rockslide. Trapped!
6. "Pulled through rift, far away. Sand, fire...windmill! On floating rock!"
8. "Through rift, again far away. Big leaves. Scary monsters—sharp, sharp teeth!"
10. "Not Nayos! Big tower; wizardy creatures. Saw small, fuzzy beasts. Broodling played with!"
12. "Far away. High, high above ground. Grassy. Hard to see—obscure! Ate 'barries,' looked sick!"
14. "Fell into rift—lots of nature, then POOF! Grew springy legs! Furry ears? Seemed confused. Ward saw from above plant house by small tower. Ask ward!
16. Return to Kosmas.
3. Find the Kryptis with explosives in the Isolated Grasslands, and use them to free Petros.
5. Find the Kryptis with explosives in the Isolated Grasslands, and use them to free Syphus.
7. Find Lali's missing toy near a sand "waterfall" and bring it to her.
9. Defeat a saurian and bring its tooth to Cadmus.
11. Find a toy for the cats in the Wizard's Tower and return to Nemea.
13. Find the correct plant at the Bastion of the Natural, and return to help cure Chloris's stomachache.
15. After speaking to the ward member, find the broodling in a forested area at the Bastion of the Natural and remind them who they are.

Riven Marl learned to whittle during an art class in Harvest Den. It is one of their favorite memories.
Riven Marl would often while away the hours with a nice, hot bath at their favorite spot in the lowlands.

Riven Marl was scouting Yagon's Gift during a period when the agony had dissipated.

Riven Marl was known to whittle high up above the Alliance Staging Ground.

8706.r: Complete each step to help Inspector Lutze solve the case.
8706.d: One person's opportunist is another's entrepreneur.

b.text: Read the letter from Chief Inspector Lutze.
Find Chief Inspector Lutze in Mantle's Arrival.
Find the crate of smuggled organs in Mantle's Arrival.
Return to Chief Inspector Lutze.
Lutze thinks there is a potential buyer among the elite of Divinity's Reach. Where do the rich like Lord Faren live again?
This is where the elite live; there must be something here.
You found a large amount of gold; looks like there is a note nearby.
The note ties Countess Cindy Breaud to the smuggling ring. Chief Inspector Lutze would like to see this.
Lutze thinks someone around Mantle's Arrival must be moving the organ crates around.
You found a crate of smuggled organs a charr was carrying, and it looks like there is a note nearby.
The note ties Saturninus Deepsmoke to the smuggling ring; Chief Inspector Lutze would like to see this.
Lutze's findings point toward a sylvari handling logistics. Question both suspects: Ylani and Isla.
You've talked to Ylani and Isla, but one is lying (or maybe both). Find a way to prove it.
Something about this report does not match up; it could have been doctored.
Something does not add up here. Return to Lutze once you figure out who is lying.
Lutze thinks there is a potential buyer around the docks in Lion's Arch.
The shipping manifests you found show Alden Wamser moving large sums of money. Interview him.
You've talked to Alden; it appears there is nothing suspicious about the money moving through Lion's Arch.
Lutze thinks some organs are making their way to Amnytas. You should poke around after helping the kodan destroy the titan organ sacs.
You've helped destroy some organs. Wait...is that someone hiding nearby?
You talked to Jaxi, but he gave nothing up. Maybe there is some evidence nearby?
The note ties Jaxi to the smuggling ring; Chief Inspector Lutze would like to see this.
You should talk to Chief Inspector Lutze about everything you found.
Lutze needs proof that the ringleader is Hedda the Cunning. Check out her former Lionguard post at Twinspur Haven.
You've made it to Twinspur Haven; there has to be something here.
You've found some hastily burned notes; putting them together you can barely make out the remnants of words: "Li-  -ch  -itt c-ve chest be-d -rone."
You found a chest with a massive amount of gold and notes from Hedda the Cunning; return to Lutze with this proof.
Lutze has what she needs to make the arrest. Good job, detective!

8752.r: Open the chest at the end of the jumping puzzle.

8753.r: Uncover the tale of a memory-seeking artifact and its true purpose. Note: You will need more than a raptor mount to get this job done.

b.text: Talk to Astral Ward Medic Miyrna at Bloodcoast Ward in Lion's Arch or at Mantle's Arrival in Bava Nisos to start.
Didn't you have to scale this Monument to have a skyscale egg experience acrophobia? Luckily, we took a small detour to look at some flowers.
Keeping your balance while carrying an explosive keg through the Depths was quite a challenge, not to mention the large wurm heads.
The only thing Hidden about this place was those branches. So many roller beetles were caught on them.
We had our own vista with a view of the Bay, the market, the hylek, and the tengu.
I am thirsty from all the walking we did, but I would not trust any of the Water here.
I couldn't participate in that fest after seeing all those cattle. I'd rather just visit a different Town and have strawberries.
When you thought you were close to the Peak, a dredge would knock you back.
The Prospect of something happening to what you call home is a scary thought.
There sure are a lot of smashed chickens, as if someone was intentionally triggering Cave-ins.
There were so many of them on that bridge, I am surprised they weren't called the Baelfire Legion.
They said "Watch the ledges," so we did. It's a nice view despite seeing the chaos in the Tundra when that ice dragon was around.
How many walls did you think were Bluffing you? I really thought this mountain was the entrance.
I don't know what I hated the most in the Woods: the spiders, the bandit with the pistol, or the skritt that threw bears.
I wanted to Run around this place but felt I was being chased by either a suspicious charr or some anomaly-looking thing.
It was peaceful here in the Meadows, but now you can get a good look at the ruins of a fort and tower.
She thought it was a False hope that we could stop the Twisted Marionette.
Getting to that vista seemed like it was another hidden Garden jumping puzzle back in our day.
If I was participating in Hunger Royale, a Wild idea would be to hang around a potato farm.
We needed to go back to the Coil and look for more clues on this "Mordremoth."
Why did they have so much garlic bread in the catacombs? Do you think we will find more in the Ruins of their city?
You took a break at some Heartwoods, but adventurers were already taking out trolls, boars, bandits, wasps, oakhearts, and spiders.
There's something surreal about hanging around inside an abandoned building, but at least you can hear Owls.
It did not feel good chasing a karka back to a Cavern where there could have been more dangers lying in wait.
If I was on the cliffs, I would be safe from any tidal waves that crashed on the Coast. It would be a disaster if you couldn't find any launch pads in time.
Using the Catacombs as a shortcut probably wasn't the best idea. It's like a maze down there.
Sometimes you were Desperate and needed to get airship support when defending your fort.
To think Mordremoth could destroy a fort so Rapidly, and now we can't use the waypoint anymore!
Have you seen Issormir? That is a large wurm. It is worth the Climb.
Return to Astral Ward Medic Miyrna at Bloodcoast Ward in Lion's Arch or at Mantle's Arrival in Bava Nisos.

I told you somewhere in Tyria, not en Route to a literal Moon. You're looking for a skritt-friend of a skritt friend, which probably is someone that exhibits skritt-like behavior, such as hoarding and appreciating shinies.

b.text: Beneath the waves, in a grotto near the village of Hullgarden, an ancient shrine rests under a tree covered with red blossoms.
A treasured addition to the hoard of shinies.
An ancient rune is hidden within a coastal enclave south of Riddled Cove. Watch out for Inquest scouts.
An old seer shrine sits atop the cliffside in the Twisting Hollows.
A seer relic is in the possession of a castaway seeking protection from the seneschal. The Hullgardeners say she usually hangs out near the Glimmering Arches.
In an underwater sanctum below Breezy Cay, an ancient tome floats among a flurry of blossoms.
Magic practically drips from the air atop the rocky cliffs of a forested grotto northeast of the Command Complex.
Out of sight and out of mind, beneath the Inquest Command Complex.
Nestled on a floating forest island north of the Priory camp in Crumbling Precipice, the ancient carvings upon this tablet escape the fate of nearby plunder.
Archival stacks of seer wisdom slowly succumb to the elements and encroaching forest in the Lost Basilica.
An ancient fountain filled with seer magic whispers quietly, deep within the Overgrown Thicket.
Ley magic courses through a grotto south of the Winding Passage. Native wavehawks use it as refuge.
A collection of your notes on possible locations to search, based on what you know thus far about the seers and Ancora.

b.text: Speak with Historian Meliza.
The remnants of a ritual can be found due south of the hunting camp in Untamed Crags.
The Inquest dug up a broken totem, but left it behind. Meliza said the dig site is northwest of the Priory's camp in Crumbling Precipice.
Meliza thought she saw an ancient plate sticking out of the dirt west of the Inquest's Command Complex. She was on her skimmer, so it was likely along the ravine.
There's an ancient ritual bowl in a clearing just north of the Enchanting Grottoes. Be wary of the medusoid stingers, though; the camps are running out of salve.
While being chased by Inquest, Meliza dropped an old ritual coin near a waterfall just east of the Command Complex. If you retrieve it, she'll be grateful.
Researchers from the Priory recovered some seer belongings near a ley-infused tree by the Twin Geysers in Tranquil Glen.
A biologist from the Astral Ward noticed some seer garb beneath a massive, ancient tree just north of the camp in Shimmering Basin.
A heavily corroded weapon is protected by enormous, hostile beetles where two streams converge east of the Priory camp in Crumbling Precipice.
Narcisse thought she saw the choya carrying an old grimoire while she was observing their dancing. What could they need it for...
Archeologists stationed at the camp near the Shimmering Basin have unearthed strange glowing rocks. Meliza would like to observe one up close.

8990.r: Learn about Castoran skimmers by helping Wavehawk Pete tend to Hullgarden's juvenile wavehawks.

b.text: Help familiarize the juveniles with their predators by tracking down a predator's tooth. These dangerous creatures typically roam Soaring Sands to the west of the Great Bewilderseed tree.
Fresh fish are staple, even for juvenile skimmers, and they can help encourage behaviors when training. Find a basket of the freshest fish south of Morningrise Prow in Jetsam Point.
Skimmers value loyalty. Find and return a package hauled by a wavehawk. The Hullgardeners say one was left near a rock between the sands and the arches.
Skimmers need frequent feedings to stay strong and healthy. Find a fishing net that needs repairs. It's submerged just south of where crabs are made kings.
Find a toy that skimmers like to play with, likely fallen to the beach from where the juvenile wavehawk was playing in the pond above.
As with all skimmers, Castoran wavehawks are affectionate—help a young one in need, and you'll receive an item as thanks. The wavehawk was last seen somewhere northwest of Morningrise Prow.
This is a detailed list of various caretaking tasks and tips on juvenile wavehawk preferences. Pete's passion for sharing his knowledge about these creatures is apparent.
